title: iria disticha
intro: Iria disticha, also known as Lippia alba or Mexican oregano, is a perennial shrub native to South America, characterized by its small, white flowers and highly aromatic leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Iria disticha include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive problems, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Iria disticha can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in cooking, particularly in Latin American and Caribbean cuisine, where its pungent flavor is often used to season meats and vegetables.
